interaction.prompt: Defensive-zone coverage. Puck is on the strong-side wall. Tap the opposing forward you should box out.

feedback.right: Net-front. As the strong-side D, the player parked in front of your goalie is your problem — get a stick on him and box him out.
feedback.wrong: The point is the LD's job. The wall is the C's job. Net-front is YOURS — that's the highest-danger area.

tip: Strong-side D in the D-zone owns the net-front. Don't get pulled to the wall — the C is there.
why: DZ coverage assigns ice. Forwards have the perimeter; defenders own the dirty areas inside the dots. Stick with your assignment, don't poach.
